frameworks.name: ecological valence theory
frameworks.one_line: preference for a stimulus reflects weighted affective valence of objects it evokes

frameworks.name: object-association account of aesthetics
frameworks.one_line: texture aesthetics extend color-preference logic via learned object associations

core_question: Can the ecological valence theory developed for color preference also predict preferences for visual textures?
key_assumptions: textures evoke associated objects/entities with affective valence
preference is predicted by a weighted average of those associations
color-preference mechanisms generalize to texture aesthetics
manipulations: varied 120 visual textures shown to participants
collected per-texture ecological associations and their affective valence
key_findings: texture preference correlated strongly with weighted average affective valence of associated objects
interpretations: supports an object-association-based account of texture aesthetic preference
ecological valence theory generalizes from color to texture
